concerns.location: spec.md:User Story 3 - Predictive Modeling and Robustness Validation
concerns.text: User Story 3 contains a broken sentence structure in Acceptance Scenario 3: 'Given the rarefaction depth threshold... The research question is: How does gut microbiome composition differ between individuals with and without diagnosed autoimmune disease? The method is: 16S rRNA gene sequencing... References: (Segata et al., 2012)...' This text appears to be a copy-paste error from a different project (autoimmune disease) and breaks the logical flow of the user story. The story's scope is unclear due to this intrusion, making it impossible to verify if FR-004, FR-005, FR-006, and FR-007 properly anchor to this specific story.

concerns.location: spec.md:User Story 3 - Predictive Modeling and Robustness Validation
concerns.text: Due to the text corruption in User Story 3 (see above), the anchoring for FR-004, FR-005, FR-006, and FR-007 is ambiguous. While they contain '(See US-3)', the content of US-3 is currently incoherent. The 'Research question' and 'Method' text inserted into the middle of the acceptance scenarios contradicts the story's stated goal of 'Predictive Modeling and Robustness Validation' for cognitive decline. This requires a rewrite of US-3 to ensure the FRs are truly anchored to a coherent story.

concerns.location: spec.md:User Story 3 - Predictive Modeling and Robustness Validation
concerns.text: The text 'References: (Segata et al., 2012) https://doi.org/10.1038/nature11230' appears abruptly in the middle of User Story 3's acceptance scenarios, interrupting the 'Given/When/Then' logic. This looks like a formatting error where a citation block was pasted into the wrong location, disrupting the story's readability and testability.
